🌊IsleLearn English XIsland Phenomena Lab · Chapter 6
Fase E · Kelas X · Explanation Text

Explanation Text:
Explaining Processes and Phenomena

Bab ini membantu kamu memahami bagaimana dan mengapa sesuatu terjadi. Kita tidak hanya membaca teori, tetapi menelusuri proses seperti hujan, pelangi, pasang surut, banjir rob, rumput laut, internet satelit, dan perlindungan terumbu karang.

Pertanyaan pemantik:
Mengapa air laut bisa pasang? Mengapa rob bisa masuk ke jalan? Bagaimana hujan terbentuk? Mengapa rumput laut bisa rusak saat air terlalu tenang? Bagaimana sinyal internet bisa sampai ke pulau?
MapFunctionGrammarVocabListeningReadingQuizWriting
Orientasi

Peta Belajar: From Question to Explanation

Explanation Text cocok untuk melatih cara berpikir ilmiah dan kritis. Siswa belajar mengamati fenomena, bertanya, menemukan sebab-akibat, menyusun urutan proses, lalu menjelaskannya dengan bahasa Inggris sederhana.

🎧ListeningMendengarkan proses/fenomena dan menangkap urutan, cause, effect, serta kata kunci.
🗣️SpeakingMenjelaskan proses secara lisan dengan kalimat pendek, jelas, dan berurutan.
📖ReadingMembaca 8 model teks dan menganalisis struktur, grammar, vocabulary, serta hubungan sebab-akibat.
🧭ViewingMembaca flowchart, diagram, infografis, dan poster proses.
✍️WritingMenulis explanation text dari template, draft, review, dan revisi.
🎤PresentingMempresentasikan proses/fenomena dengan visual support.
🧪Grammar LabSimple Present, Passive Voice, connector, sequencing, dan technical words.
📱Digital CreationMembuat poster, flowchart, carousel, script voice-over, atau infografis.
Tujuan Pembelajaran

Learning Objectives

ElemenYang Siswa Kuasai
ListeningMemahami gagasan utama, detail, urutan proses, dan sebab-akibat dari penjelasan lisan tentang fenomena.
SpeakingMenjelaskan proses/fenomena lokal secara sederhana dengan pola first–then–as a result–finally.
ReadingMengenali general statement, sequenced explanation, closing, connector, passive voice, dan technical vocabulary.
ViewingMengubah diagram/flowchart/poster menjadi kalimat explanation text.
WritingMenulis explanation text pendek sampai menengah dengan simple present, passive voice, cause–effect connector, dan vocabulary tepat.
PresentingMempresentasikan fenomena dengan struktur jelas, visual support, pronunciation cukup jelas, dan confidence.
Core Concept

What Is Explanation Text?

Explanation Text adalah teks yang menjelaskan how something happens atau why something happens. Teks ini tidak hanya menyebutkan langkah, tetapi menjelaskan hubungan antara proses, sebab, dan akibat.

Rumus berpikir Explanation Text: Phenomenon → Causes / Process → Effects / Result → Why it matters.
Explanation

Menjelaskan bagaimana/mengapa sesuatu terjadi.

Example: How rain forms.
Procedure

Memberi instruksi cara melakukan sesuatu.

Example: How to make tea.
Descriptive

Menggambarkan orang, benda, atau tempat.

Example: My school is near the sea.
Recount

Menceritakan pengalaman masa lalu.

Example: Yesterday, we harvested seaweed.
Text Structure

Structure of Explanation Text

General Statement

Memperkenalkan fenomena/proses yang akan dijelaskan. Biasanya menjawab: What is it? atau What phenomenon is being explained?

Rob flooding is coastal flooding caused by seawater moving inland.

Sequenced Explanation

Menjelaskan urutan proses atau rangkaian cause–effect. Bagian ini adalah inti teks. Gunakan connector agar ide tidak loncat-loncat.

First... Then... Because... As a result...

Closing / Impact

Menutup dengan kesimpulan, dampak, atau alasan mengapa fenomena itu penting dipahami.

Therefore, understanding tides helps people prepare safer routes.
Visual Learning

Flowcharts, Diagrams, Infographics, and Process Posters

Gambar-gambar berikut sudah dimasukkan satu per satu ke dalam materi. Gunakan gambar ini untuk predicting, viewing, reading support, speaking practice, dan digital creation model.

Deep Grammar Lab

Grammar Lab: Build Strong Explanation Sentences

Untuk siswa pemula, grammar tidak perlu dimulai dari istilah yang rumit. Mulailah dari tiga pertanyaan: Apa yang terjadi? Mengapa terjadi? Apa akibatnya?

1) Simple Present Tense — for General Facts

Explanation Text sering memakai Simple Present karena menjelaskan fakta umum atau proses yang biasanya terjadi.

Subject + Verb 1 / Verb-s/es + Object/Complement
Water evaporates when the sun heats it.
The sea level rises during high tide.
Seaweed grows better with sunlight and nutrients.
Coral reefs reduce wave energy.
Satellites send signals to receivers on Earth.

Mini Pattern

Gunakan pola ini saat siswa masih bingung:

Something happens + because + reason.
Rain falls because clouds become heavy.
Rob flooding happens because sea water moves inland.
Seaweed becomes unhealthy because the water is too still.

2) Passive Voice — Focus on the Process, Not the Doer

Passive voice dipakai ketika yang penting adalah benda/proses yang terjadi, bukan siapa pelakunya.

Subject + am/is/are + Verb 3
Subject + am/is/are + Verb 3 + by + doer
Water is heated by the sun.
Water vapor is carried by warm air.
Seaweed seedlings are tied to ropes.
The signal is sent to satellites.
Wave energy is reduced by coral reefs.

Active → Passive Slowly

Active: The sun heats water.
Passive: Water is heated by the sun.
Active: Farmers tie seaweed to ropes.
Passive: Seaweed is tied to ropes.
Active: Satellites send signals.
Passive: Signals are sent by satellites.
Active: Coral reefs reduce wave energy.
Passive: Wave energy is reduced by coral reefs.

3) Cause and Effect Connectors

Connector membantu siswa menjelaskan alasan dan akibat. Tanpa connector, teks hanya terasa seperti daftar kalimat.

because+ sentence/reason
Rain falls because clouds become heavy.
because of+ noun phrase
Flooding happens because of high tide.
due to+ noun phrase
Seaweed can be damaged due to calm water.
as a resulteffect sentence
The sea level rises. As a result, roads can flood.
thereforeconclusion/effect
The cloud is heavy; therefore, rain falls.
this causeseffect
Weak drainage blocks water. This causes flooding to last longer.
this leads toresult
Rain wets drying seaweed. This leads to lower quality.
sosimple effect
The land is low, so seawater enters easily.

4) Sequencing Words

BeginningFirst, the process begins when, at the beginning, initially.
MiddleThen, next, after that, while, during, as the process continues.
EndingFinally, in the end, as a result, therefore, this process is important because.
Vocabulary Bank

Words for Explaining Phenomena

Kosakata ini disusun agar siswa punya bahan untuk menjelaskan proses alam, laut/cuaca, teknologi, rumput laut, dan kehidupan kepulauan.

WordMeaningSimple ExampleLocal / Context Example
evaporatemenguapWater evaporates when the sun heats it.Sea water evaporates faster during hot days in Sapuka.
condensemengembunWater vapor condenses into tiny droplets.Moist air condenses into clouds above the sea.
precipitationcurah hujan / air yang jatuh dari awanRain is a type of precipitation.Heavy precipitation can fill water tanks in the rainy season.
water vaporuap airWater vapor is invisible gas in the air.Warm sea water can produce water vapor.
droplettetesan kecilClouds are made of tiny droplets.Droplets join together before rain falls.
gravitygaya tarik bumi/benda langitGravity pulls objects toward Earth.The moon’s gravity helps create tides.
tidepasang surutTides happen every day.Students may see the shore change during high tide.
high tideair pasangHigh tide raises sea level.Rob flooding is worse during very high tide.
low tideair surutLow tide exposes more beach.People can walk farther on the shore during low tide.
coastalpesisirCoastal areas are near the sea.Sapuka has coastal villages.
floodingbanjirFlooding covers roads with water.Rob flooding can disturb school access.
shorelinegaris pantaiThe shoreline is where land meets the sea.Waves reach the shoreline during high tide.
erosionabrasi / pengikisanErosion removes soil or sand.Strong waves can cause coastal erosion.
sea levelpermukaan lautSea level can rise during high tide.Low areas are affected when sea level rises.
drainagesaluran airGood drainage helps water flow away.Weak drainage makes rob flooding last longer.
seedlingbibitA seedling is a young plant.Small seaweed seedlings are tied to ropes.
nutrientnutrisiPlants need nutrients to grow.Seaweed gets nutrients from seawater.
harvestmemanenFarmers harvest mature seaweed.Families harvest seaweed when it is ready.
dryingpengeringanDrying removes water from seaweed.Rain during drying can reduce seaweed quality.
qualitymutu/kualitasGood drying improves quality.Wet seaweed may have lower quality.
signalsinyalA signal carries information.Internet signals are sent by satellites.
satellitesatelitA satellite moves in space.Starlink satellites send internet signals.
receiverpenerima sinyalA receiver catches the signal.A school receiver gets the signal from satellites.
deviceperangkatPhones and laptops are devices.Students use devices to access learning materials.
coral reefterumbu karangA coral reef is a living structure in shallow sea.Coral reefs help protect small islands.
wave energyenergi ombakWave energy becomes weaker at the reef.Reefs reduce wave energy before waves reach the shore.
protectmelindungiReefs protect coasts from strong waves.Mangroves and reefs can protect island communities.
processprosesA process has steps or stages.Explanation texts show the process clearly.
phenomenonfenomenaA phenomenon is something that happens in nature or society.Tides are natural phenomena.
causepenyebabA cause is the reason something happens.Strong wind can be a cause of flooding.
effectakibat/dampakAn effect is the result of a cause.Flooded roads are an effect of rob flooding.
resulthasil/akibatRain is the result of a water cycle process.As a result, students may walk through water.
thereforeoleh karena ituThe cloud is heavy; therefore, rain falls.The water rises; therefore, low roads can flood.
becausekarenaRain falls because clouds become heavy.Seaweed grows well because it gets sunlight.
because ofkarena + kata bendaFlooding happens because of high tide.Seaweed quality drops because of rain during drying.
due todisebabkan olehRob flooding can happen due to high tide.Seaweed can be damaged due to calm water.
as a resultakibatnyaThe sea level rises. As a result, water enters the road.The seaweed gets wet again. As a result, quality becomes lower.
firstpertamaFirst, the sun heats water.First, farmers prepare ropes.
thenkemudianThen, water evaporates.Then, seedlings are tied to ropes.
after thatsetelah ituAfter that, clouds form.After that, the ropes are placed in the sea.
finallyakhirnyaFinally, rain falls.Finally, seaweed is dried under the sun.
whilesementara/ketikaWhile the sun heats water, evaporation happens.While seaweed dries, rain can make it wet again.
whenketikaWhen droplets become heavy, rain falls.When high tide comes, sea water can move inland.
risenaikWater vapor rises into the sky.Sea level rises during high tide.
flowmengalirWater flows back to the sea.Flood water flows into low roads.
absorbmenyerapDry seaweed absorbs water when rain falls.The ground absorbs some water after rain.
reducemengurangiCoral reefs reduce wave energy.Good drainage can reduce flooding.
reflectmemantulkanLight reflects inside a raindrop.A rainbow forms when light is reflected and refracted.
refractmembiaskanLight refracts when it enters water.Sunlight refracts inside raindrops.
Listening Zone

Listen and Catch the Process

Gunakan tombol TTS untuk latihan mandiri. Jika suara TTS tidak berjalan di perangkat tertentu, guru dapat membaca transcript dan siswa tetap mengerjakan tugas listening.

1. Short Listening: How Rain Forms

Listen to this short explanation. The sun heats water in the sea. The water changes into water vapor and rises into the air. When the vapor reaches cooler air, it becomes tiny droplets. The droplets form clouds. When the clouds are heavy, rain falls to the earth.

Before Listening
  1. Look at the title. Predict the process or cause.
  2. Write 3 words you expect to hear.
While Listening
  1. Write the first stage you hear.
  2. Write one cause and one effect.
  3. Write one connector you hear.
After Listening
  1. Retell the process in 3–4 sentences.
  2. Explain one difficult word to a friend.

2. Medium Listening: Why Tides Happen

Tides are the rise and fall of sea level. They happen mainly because of the moon’s gravity. The moon pulls ocean water, so the water rises in some places. These rises are called high tides. Between the high tides, sea level is lower. These areas have low tide. The sun also affects tides, but the moon has a stronger effect.

Before Listening
  1. Look at the title. Predict the process or cause.
  2. Write 3 words you expect to hear.
While Listening
  1. Write the first stage you hear.
  2. Write one cause and one effect.
  3. Write one connector you hear.
After Listening
  1. Retell the process in 3–4 sentences.
  2. Explain one difficult word to a friend.

3. Longer Listening: How Seaweed Grows

Seaweed farming begins when farmers prepare ropes. Small seaweed seedlings are tied to the ropes. Then the ropes are placed in seawater and fixed in place. Seaweed needs sunlight, nutrients, clean water, and gentle movement. After several weeks, the seaweed becomes bigger. Farmers harvest it and dry it under the sun. If rain falls during drying, the quality may become lower.

Before Listening
  1. Look at the title. Predict the process or cause.
  2. Write 3 words you expect to hear.
While Listening
  1. Write the first stage you hear.
  2. Write one cause and one effect.
  3. Write one connector you hear.
After Listening
  1. Retell the process in 3–4 sentences.
  2. Explain one difficult word to a friend.

4. Dialogue Listening: Why Rob Flooding Happens

Alya: Why is the road wet this morning?
Rafi: It is rob flooding. Sea water came into the road during high tide.
Alya: Why did the water move inland?
Rafi: The land is low, and the tide was high. Strong wind also pushed water toward the shore.
Alya: So the causes worked together?
Rafi: Yes. That is why rob flooding can happen in coastal areas.

Before Listening
  1. Look at the title. Predict the process or cause.
  2. Write 3 words you expect to hear.
While Listening
  1. Write the first stage you hear.
  2. Write one cause and one effect.
  3. Write one connector you hear.
After Listening
  1. Retell the process in 3–4 sentences.
  2. Explain one difficult word to a friend.
Speaking Zone

Speak Like a Phenomenon Explorer

Pair TalkAsk and answer: “How does it happen?” “Why does it happen?” “What happens next?”
Explain with PicturesChoose one infographic. Explain the process in 5–7 sentences.
Cause–Effect ChainStudent A says a cause. Student B says the effect. Continue the chain.
One-Minute TalkExplain one local phenomenon: tides, seaweed growth, rob flooding, or rain.

Dialogue Model: Why Seaweed Can Grow Well or Get Damaged

Naya: Why does seaweed grow well in some months?

Rafi: It grows well because it gets enough sunlight, clean water, and gentle water movement.

Naya: Why is water movement important?

Rafi: Moving water brings fresh nutrients and keeps the seaweed clean.

Naya: What happens when the water is too calm?

Rafi: Unwanted growth may appear. As a result, the seaweed can become weak or unhealthy.

Naya: What about rain during drying?

Rafi: Rain makes the seaweed wet again. This can lower its quality.

Reading Zone

8 Model Explanation Texts

Setiap teks dilengkapi gambar, process chain, glossary, comprehension, structure analysis, dan reflection. Siswa tidak hanya membaca, tetapi melihat pola berpikir explanation.

Text 1Short TextExplanation Model

How Rain Forms

How Rain FormsGunakan gambar unik yang sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Learning. Fokuskan pembacaan teks pada urutan proses, hubungan sebab-akibat, dan connector yang dipakai.

Rain forms through the water cycle. This cycle explains how water moves from the sea, land, and rivers into the air and returns to the earth as rain. The process begins when the sun heats water on the surface of oceans, rivers, lakes, and wet land. When water gets enough heat, it changes into water vapor. This process is called evaporation. The vapor is lighter than liquid water, so it rises into the air. Higher in the sky, the air is cooler. The vapor cools and condenses into tiny water droplets. These droplets gather and form clouds. When more droplets join together, the clouds become heavy. Finally, the water falls back to the earth as rain. Some water flows over the land into rivers and the sea, and some water soaks into the ground. In island areas, rain is very important because many families collect rainwater for daily use.

Cause–Effect / Process Chain:
sun heats water → water evaporates → vapor rises → vapor condenses → clouds become heavy → rain falls
Key Words: evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection
Comprehension
  1. What phenomenon/process is explained?
  2. What happens first?
  3. What is the final result?
  4. Mention two important vocabulary items from the text.
Structure Analysis
  1. Underline the general statement.
  2. Circle two sequence connectors.
  3. Box one cause connector and one effect sentence.
  4. Find one sentence in passive voice if available.
Reflection

How is this phenomenon connected to daily life in Sapuka or other coastal areas?

Text 2Short TextExplanation Model

How a Rainbow Forms

How a Rainbow FormsGunakan gambar unik yang sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Learning. Fokuskan pembacaan teks pada urutan proses, hubungan sebab-akibat, dan connector yang dipakai.

A rainbow is a natural phenomenon that appears when sunlight meets raindrops in the air. It usually happens when the sun shines while rain is still falling or when there are many small water droplets in the air. First, sunlight enters a raindrop. Sunlight may look white, but it actually contains many colors. When the light enters the drop, it bends. This bending is called refraction. Inside the drop, the light is separated into different colors. Then the light reflects from the back of the drop. After that, it leaves the raindrop and bends again. The colors spread out and travel toward our eyes. As a result, we can see red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet in the sky. A rainbow is not an object that we can touch. It is an optical effect created by sunlight, water droplets, and the position of the observer.

Cause–Effect / Process Chain:
sunlight enters raindrop → light bends → colors separate → light reflects → light reaches eyes
Key Words: sunlight, raindrop, refraction, reflection
Comprehension
  1. What phenomenon/process is explained?
  2. What happens first?
  3. What is the final result?
  4. Mention two important vocabulary items from the text.
Structure Analysis
  1. Underline the general statement.
  2. Circle two sequence connectors.
  3. Box one cause connector and one effect sentence.
  4. Find one sentence in passive voice if available.
Reflection

How is this phenomenon connected to daily life in Sapuka or other coastal areas?

Text 3Medium TextExplanation Model

Why Tides Happen

Why Tides HappenGunakan gambar unik yang sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Learning. Fokuskan pembacaan teks pada urutan proses, hubungan sebab-akibat, dan connector yang dipakai.

Tides are the regular rise and fall of sea level. They happen because the moon and the sun pull on Earth’s ocean water. The moon has the strongest effect because it is much closer to Earth than the sun. When the moon’s gravity pulls ocean water, the water rises on the side of Earth facing the moon. Another rise also happens on the opposite side. These two rises create high tides. Areas between the two high tides have lower sea level, called low tide. The sun also pulls on the ocean, but its effect is weaker. When the sun, Earth, and moon line up, tides can become higher than usual. For people living near the sea, tides are important to understand. Fishermen, seaweed farmers, and students who walk near the shore can be affected by high tide and low tide.

Cause–Effect / Process Chain:
moon’s gravity pulls ocean water → sea level rises in two areas → high tide happens → areas between them have low tide
Key Words: gravity, high tide, low tide, sea level
Comprehension
  1. What phenomenon/process is explained?
  2. What happens first?
  3. What is the final result?
  4. Mention two important vocabulary items from the text.
Structure Analysis
  1. Underline the general statement.
  2. Circle two sequence connectors.
  3. Box one cause connector and one effect sentence.
  4. Find one sentence in passive voice if available.
Reflection

How is this phenomenon connected to daily life in Sapuka or other coastal areas?

Text 4Medium TextExplanation Model

Why Rob Flooding Happens

Why Rob Flooding HappensGunakan gambar unik yang sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Learning. Fokuskan pembacaan teks pada urutan proses, hubungan sebab-akibat, dan connector yang dipakai.

Rob flooding is coastal flooding caused by sea water moving inland. It often happens in low coastal areas when sea level becomes higher than the land or road surface. Several factors can work together. First, high tide raises the sea water level. Second, low land is easier for sea water to reach. Third, strong winds can push sea water toward the shore. Fourth, sea level rise can make coastal flooding happen more often. Weak drainage can also make the water stay longer because the water cannot flow away easily. When these factors happen at the same time, sea water moves over the shoreline, enters roads, and sometimes reaches houses or school paths. As a result, roads become wet, people find it difficult to walk, and saltwater can make the ground salty. In coastal villages, understanding rob flooding helps people prepare safer routes and protect important belongings.

Cause–Effect / Process Chain:
high tide + low land + wind + weak drainage → seawater moves inland → roads/houses/school paths are affected
Key Words: rob flooding, high tide, low land, drainage
Comprehension
  1. What phenomenon/process is explained?
  2. What happens first?
  3. What is the final result?
  4. Mention two important vocabulary items from the text.
Structure Analysis
  1. Underline the general statement.
  2. Circle two sequence connectors.
  3. Box one cause connector and one effect sentence.
  4. Find one sentence in passive voice if available.
Reflection

How is this phenomenon connected to daily life in Sapuka or other coastal areas?

Text 5Medium TextExplanation Model

How Seaweed Grows

How Seaweed GrowsGunakan gambar unik yang sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Learning. Fokuskan pembacaan teks pada urutan proses, hubungan sebab-akibat, dan connector yang dipakai.

Seaweed farming is a common activity in many island communities. Seaweed grows in seawater and needs sunlight, nutrients, clean water, and gentle water movement. The process begins when farmers prepare strong ropes. Small seaweed seedlings are tied to the ropes at regular spaces. Then the ropes are placed in the sea and fixed so they do not drift away. In the water, the seaweed receives sunlight from above and nutrients from seawater. Gentle water movement brings fresh nutrients and keeps the seaweed cleaner. Day by day, the seaweed becomes bigger and stronger. When it is mature, farmers harvest it carefully from the ropes. After harvesting, the seaweed is usually washed and dried under the sun. Proper drying is important because wet seaweed can lose quality. This process shows how people use natural conditions and careful work to produce seaweed.

Cause–Effect / Process Chain:
prepare ropes → tie seedlings → place ropes in sea → seaweed gets sunlight/nutrients → harvest → dry
Key Words: seedling, nutrients, harvest, drying
Comprehension
  1. What phenomenon/process is explained?
  2. What happens first?
  3. What is the final result?
  4. Mention two important vocabulary items from the text.
Structure Analysis
  1. Underline the general statement.
  2. Circle two sequence connectors.
  3. Box one cause connector and one effect sentence.
  4. Find one sentence in passive voice if available.
Reflection

How is this phenomenon connected to daily life in Sapuka or other coastal areas?

Text 6Longer TextExplanation Model

Why Seaweed Can Be Damaged by Calm Water or Rain During Drying

Why Seaweed Can Be Damaged by Calm Water or Rain During DryingGunakan gambar unik yang sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Learning. Fokuskan pembacaan teks pada urutan proses, hubungan sebab-akibat, dan connector yang dipakai.

Seaweed can grow well when the sea conditions are suitable, but it can also be damaged by certain conditions. One problem is very calm water. Seaweed needs gentle movement because moving water brings fresh nutrients and helps keep the seaweed clean. If the water is too still for a long time, algae or other unwanted organisms may grow on the seaweed. This can make the seaweed weaker, slower to grow, or even rotten. Another problem happens after harvest. Seaweed is often dried under the sun to remove water. However, if rain falls while the seaweed is drying, it becomes wet again. The seaweed absorbs water from the rain. As a result, the quality can become lower. It may change color, smell bad, or dry unevenly. Therefore, seaweed farmers need to watch the weather, choose a good drying place, and protect the seaweed from sudden rain.

Cause–Effect / Process Chain:
too calm water → unwanted growth → weak seaweed; rain during drying → wet seaweed → lower quality
Key Words: calm water, algae, absorb, quality
Comprehension
  1. What phenomenon/process is explained?
  2. What happens first?
  3. What is the final result?
  4. Mention two important vocabulary items from the text.
Structure Analysis
  1. Underline the general statement.
  2. Circle two sequence connectors.
  3. Box one cause connector and one effect sentence.
  4. Find one sentence in passive voice if available.
Reflection

How is this phenomenon connected to daily life in Sapuka or other coastal areas?

Text 7Longer TextExplanation Model

How Starlink Internet Works in Remote Islands

How Starlink Internet Works in Remote IslandsGunakan gambar unik yang sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Learning. Fokuskan pembacaan teks pada urutan proses, hubungan sebab-akibat, dan connector yang dipakai.

Starlink is a satellite internet system that can help remote areas connect to the internet. In simple terms, internet data is sent through satellites in space and received by a special dish on the ground. The process starts from internet network stations on Earth. The signal is sent to satellites that move above the planet. These satellites pass the signal through space. Then a receiver dish at a house, school, or office catches the signal. After that, a router shares the internet connection with devices such as phones, laptops, and tablets. In remote islands, this process is useful because cable internet and regular mobile networks may be limited. With satellite internet, students can access learning materials, teachers can communicate more easily, and communities can stay connected. However, internet speed can still be affected by weather, electricity, equipment position, and the number of users.

Cause–Effect / Process Chain:
network station → satellites → receiver dish → router → devices connect
Key Words: satellite, receiver, signal, device
Comprehension
  1. What phenomenon/process is explained?
  2. What happens first?
  3. What is the final result?
  4. Mention two important vocabulary items from the text.
Structure Analysis
  1. Underline the general statement.
  2. Circle two sequence connectors.
  3. Box one cause connector and one effect sentence.
  4. Find one sentence in passive voice if available.
Reflection

How is this phenomenon connected to daily life in Sapuka or other coastal areas?

Text 8Longer TextExplanation Model

How Coral Reefs Protect Small Islands

How Coral Reefs Protect Small IslandsGunakan gambar unik yang sudah tersedia di bagian Visual Learning. Fokuskan pembacaan teks pada urutan proses, hubungan sebab-akibat, dan connector yang dipakai.

Coral reefs are living structures found in shallow tropical seas. They are important because they protect small islands from strong waves. When waves travel from the deep sea, they carry energy. If there is no barrier, strong waves can hit the shoreline directly and cause erosion. Coral reefs act like natural barriers. When waves reach the reef, part of the wave energy is broken and reduced. After passing the reef, the water near the shore becomes calmer. As a result, the shoreline gets more protection and erosion can be reduced. Coral reefs also support sea life because many fish and small marine animals live there. For island communities, healthy reefs are valuable because they help protect coasts, support fishing, and maintain a healthy marine environment. Therefore, coral reefs need to be protected from destructive fishing, pollution, and careless activities.

Cause–Effect / Process Chain:
strong wave → hits coral reef → wave energy is reduced → calmer shoreline → less erosion
Key Words: coral reef, wave energy, shoreline, erosion
Comprehension
  1. What phenomenon/process is explained?
  2. What happens first?
  3. What is the final result?
  4. Mention two important vocabulary items from the text.
Structure Analysis
  1. Underline the general statement.
  2. Circle two sequence connectors.
  3. Box one cause connector and one effect sentence.
  4. Find one sentence in passive voice if available.
Reflection

How is this phenomenon connected to daily life in Sapuka or other coastal areas?

Viewing Zone

Read Diagrams, Flowcharts, and Infographics

Viewing Task 1: Flowchart
  1. Choose “How Rain Forms”.
  2. Write each stage in your notebook.
  3. Turn the flowchart into one paragraph using first, then, after that, finally.
Viewing Task 2: Cause–Effect Map
  1. Choose “Why Rob Flooding Happens”.
  2. Write five causes and four effects.
  3. Make two sentences using because and as a result.
Viewing Task 3: Process Poster
  1. Choose “How Seaweed Grows”.
  2. Explain the process orally to a partner.
  3. Use at least three passive voice sentences.
Viewing Task 4: Science Diagram
  1. Choose “How a Rainbow Forms”.
  2. Find the words refraction and reflection.
  3. Explain the difference in simple Indonesian first, then English.
Annotated Model Text

Why Rob Flooding Happens in Coastal Villages

Perhatikan warna anotasi. Ini membantu siswa melihat fungsi setiap bagian kalimat.

General Statement Rob flooding is coastal flooding that happens when sea water moves inland and reaches low coastal areas.

Sequence First, the sea level rises during high tide. Cause This happens because the moon’s gravity affects ocean water. Effect As a result, the water becomes higher than usual.

Sequence Then, strong winds can push the water toward the shoreline. Passive Voice Low roads and houses can be reached by seawater when the land is lower than the sea level.

Cause If drainage is weak, the water cannot flow away quickly. Effect Therefore, roads stay flooded for a longer time, and students may find it difficult to go to school safely.

Technical Vocabulary High tide, sea level, shoreline, drainage, and coastal flooding are important words in this explanation.

Closing Understanding rob flooding helps coastal communities prepare safer routes and protect their homes.

Common Mistakes Clinic

Fix Common Mistakes

❌ Water evaporated when the sun heats it.
✅ Water evaporates when the sun heats it.
💡 Explanation Text uses Simple Present for general processes.
❌ Because high tide, the road floods.
✅ Because of high tide, the road floods.
💡 Use because of before a noun phrase.
❌ Water vapor is rise into the sky.
✅ Water vapor rises into the sky.
💡 “Rise” is an active verb here. Do not use “is” before Verb 1.
❌ The signal sent by satellites.
✅ The signal is sent by satellites.
💡 Passive voice needs be + Verb 3.
❌ First prepare ropes. Then tie seaweed. Then put it in the sea.
✅ First, ropes are prepared. Then, seaweed seedlings are tied to the ropes because they need a place to grow.
💡 Explanation Text explains reasons, not only steps.
❌ Rob flooding is happen because sea high.
✅ Rob flooding happens because the sea level is high.
💡 Use correct verb and complete noun phrase: sea level.
Guided Practice

Practice Step by Step

A. Identify the Structure

Read one model text. Mark: General Statement, Sequenced Explanation, Closing.

B. Match Cause and Effect

CauseHigh tide
Calm water
Rain during drying
Coral reef barrier
EffectRoads can flood
Seaweed becomes unhealthy
Seaweed quality becomes lower
Wave energy is reduced

C. Arrange the Process

Arrange these stages: rain falls / vapor rises / clouds become heavy / sun heats water / water evaporates / vapor condenses.

D. Change Active to Passive

1. The sun heats water. 2. Farmers tie seaweed to ropes. 3. Satellites send signals. 4. Coral reefs reduce wave energy.

1. Water is heated by the sun. 2. Seaweed is tied to ropes by farmers. 3. Signals are sent by satellites. 4. Wave energy is reduced by coral reefs.

E. Add Connectors

Complete with because, because of, as a result, therefore, or after that.

  1. The land is low, ____ seawater can enter easily.
  2. Flooding happens ____ high tide.
  3. Clouds become heavy. ____, rain falls.
  4. Farmers prepare ropes. ____, seedlings are tied to them.
Interactive Quiz

Explanation Text Quiz

Jawab 15 soal. Setelah memilih jawaban, kamu akan mendapat feedback singkat.

Question 1

What is the main purpose of an Explanation Text?

Explanation Text explains processes, causes, effects, and phenomena.
Question 2

Which structure is correct for Explanation Text?

Explanation Text begins by introducing a phenomenon, explains the process, then closes with a conclusion or impact.
Question 3

“Water vapor rises because it is lighter than air.” Which word shows cause?

Because connects the effect with the reason/cause.
Question 4

Choose the best passive voice sentence.

Passive voice uses be + V3: water is heated by the sun.
Question 5

Which sentence uses Simple Present correctly?

Explanation Text often uses Simple Present for general facts: Seaweed grows in seawater.
Question 6

What happens after water vapor cools?

Cooling vapor condenses into tiny droplets that form clouds.
Question 7

Which phrase is best for a noun cause? “Flooding happens ___ high tide.”

Because of is followed by a noun phrase, such as high tide.
Question 8

Which is a sequence connector?

After that helps order the stages in a process.
Question 9

In tides, what has the strongest effect on sea level?

The moon’s gravity is the main cause of tides because the moon is close to Earth.
Question 10

What can coral reefs reduce before waves reach the shore?

Coral reefs can reduce wave energy, so the shoreline becomes calmer.
Question 11

Why can rain damage seaweed during drying?

Rain wets drying seaweed again, so quality can become lower.
Question 12

Which sentence explains an effect?

As a result introduces an effect or consequence.
Question 13

Which topic is suitable for Explanation Text?

How Rain Forms explains a natural process. How to Make Tea is Procedure Text.
Question 14

Choose the best connector: “The water is too still. ___, unwanted organisms grow on the seaweed.”

As a result connects a cause to an effect.
Question 15

Which sentence is NOT an Explanation Text sentence?

“Then click the login button” is an instruction, so it belongs more to Procedure Text.

Your Score

0/15

Writing Mission

Write Your Own Explanation Text

BeginnerWrite 8–10 sentences. Use first, then, because, as a result, finally.
IntermediateWrite 2–3 paragraphs, 120–180 words. Include structure and passive voice.
ChallengeWrite 200–250 words with technical vocabulary, cause–effect chain, and local context.

Topic Choices

How rain forms
Why tides happen
How seaweed grows
Why rob flooding happens
How Starlink helps remote learning
How coral reefs protect islands
Why clean water becomes difficult in dry season
Why seaweed quality can become lower

Writing Template

Title: __________

Paragraph 1 — General Statement: This text explains how/why ...

Paragraph 2 — Sequenced Explanation: The process begins when ... Then ... This happens because ... As a result ...

Paragraph 3 — Closing / Impact: This phenomenon is important because ...

0 words

Self-Editing Checklist

Presenting Zone

Present Your Explanation

Presentation Script Template

Good morning. Today, I will explain ...

This phenomenon happens because ...

First, ... Then, ... After that, ...

As a result, ...

In conclusion, this phenomenon is important because ...

Simple Rubric

AspectGood Performance
ContentAccurate process and cause-effect explanation.
StructureClear opening, sequence, and closing.
VocabularyUses technical words correctly.
PronunciationMostly clear and understandable.
ConfidenceSpeaks with preparation and eye contact.
VisualUses diagram/poster/flowchart to support explanation.
Digital Creation Lab

Create a Digital Explanation Product

InfographicCreate a cause-effect infographic about rob flooding or seaweed damage.
Carousel PostMake 5 slides: title, cause, process, effect, closing.
Short Video ScriptWrite a 45–60 second voice-over explaining one phenomenon.

Sample Carousel Text: Why Tides Happen

Slide 1: Why does sea level rise and fall?

Slide 2: The moon’s gravity pulls ocean water.

Slide 3: Water rises on two sides of Earth. These are high tides.

Slide 4: Areas between them have low tide.

Slide 5: Understanding tides helps coastal communities plan safer activities.

Teacher Notes

Teaching Notes for 2–4 Meetings

Meeting 1Start from local questions and visuals. Compare Explanation vs Procedure. Introduce structure.
Meeting 2Use Grammar Lab slowly. Practice simple present, passive voice, and connectors using local sentences.
Meeting 3Read 2–4 model texts. Students identify structure, cause-effect chain, and vocabulary.
Meeting 4Writing mission, peer review, and presentation using posters/flowcharts.
For Weak StudentsGive sentence starters, word bank, picture support, and pair work. Accept short but correct sentences first.
Offline ClassPrint selected images or show from teacher phone/laptop. TTS can be replaced by teacher reading aloud.
Final Reflection

From Seeing Phenomena to Explaining Them

Setelah bab ini, siswa diharapkan tidak hanya tahu definisi Explanation Text, tetapi mampu bertanya, mengamati, menyusun proses, menjelaskan sebab-akibat, dan menghubungkan materi Bahasa Inggris dengan kehidupan nyata di wilayah kepulauan.

IsleLearn English X · Chapter 6 Explanation Text · Offline-first HTML learning module
